Kratom (Mitragyna speciosa) is a tropical evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia, known for its medicinal and psychoactive properties. The health benefits of Kratom include pain relief, anxiety and stress reduction, and improved sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Kratom include alkaloids such as mitragynine, 7-hydroxymitragynine, and paynanthine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Kratom is often prepared as herbal teas, capsules, or extracts, and is also used in traditional medicine in Southeast Asia, where it is commonly consumed as a fresh leaf tea or added to traditional remedies.
